content.content: {"result":"{\"conversation_id\": \"conv_7e3a9d1c5f8b\", \"title\": \"Clint Peterson & Reshma Patel\", \"participant_ids\": [\"Clint Peterson\", \"Reshma Patel\"], \"last_updated\": \"2026-06-09T14:30:00Z\", \"messages\": [{\"message_id\": \"msg_a7d3c1e9b5f2\", \"sender_id\": \"Reshma Patel\", \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-09T11:00:00Z\", \"content\": \"Hey Clint, about the May 28 CPE session. I originally registered 14 people but three cancelled after the registration deadline: Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, and Steven Perry. I'll run the state portal checks on the Texas items and compare them to the binder receipts. If the record is clean, I'll mark them ready for filing prep; if not, I'll leave them in hold.", "user": "persona_015", "thread_ts": "1779723040.439968"}, {"ts": "1779723660.873301", "type": "message", "text": "The working direction is to proceed with any overdue annual report where the entity is confirmed active and the supporting records are complete. Anaya's note on missing foreign qualification dates is material, so keep those separated until resolved. We should not overstate status on any item that remains unverified.", "user": "persona_016", "thread_ts": "1779722960.415012", "reply_count": 1, "reply_users_count": 1, "latest_reply": "1779723840.969624"}, {"ts": "1779723550.749842", "type": "message", "text": "That mismatch needs to stay in the exception log until we verify it with the portal screenshot or filing receipt. Please do not collapse it into the cleared set yet; we need a defensible record.", "user": "persona_005", "thread_ts": "1779723480.745000"}, {"ts": "1779723480.745000", "type": "message", "text": "I've got two entities where the last-year annual report was marked filed, but the current-year state portal still shows pending due to a name mismatch. I'm checking whether that's just portal lag or a real follow-up item.", "user": "persona_013", "reply_count": 1, "reply_users_count": 1, "latest_reply": "1779723550.749842"}, {"ts": "1779723380.596030", "type": "message", "text": "@emily.adekole can you sanity-check the entity list against the compliance binder and confirm whether any of the overdue items are actually dissolved or inactive? That would change the priority order.", "user": "persona_001", "thread_ts": "1779723300.531757"}, {"ts": "1779723300.531757", "type": "message", "text": "One caution: for entities with prior-year lapse history, we should record the reason for delay in the internal file, even if the report itself is straightforward. That protects the audit trail and keeps the remediation narrative consistent if there is later regulator inquiry.", "user": "persona_005", "reply_count": 1, "reply_users_count": 1, "latest_reply": "1779723380.596030"}, {"ts": "1779723190.474706", "type": "message", "text": "Agreed. I thought we promoted internally to fill that gap.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780148700.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Clean diagnosis on VEN-030-736427 / apinv_e42396e9bdd64294: treat it as stale and void on our side. Owen, please append the write-up to the existing CrownPeak follow-up Linear ticket so all three items live on one record, and loop Priya in on the cancellation-trigger process gap when you do.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780069320.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I found the northstar_legal correspondence in the engagement file. CrownPeak and Northstar cancelled the Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house, which is about eleven days before this pre-bill was actually issued on 2026-03-11. I do not see any credit memo in file, and the original pre-bill was never voided on either side, so this looks like the first clear breadcrumb that the obligation was extinguished before the invoice even left CrownPeak's billing system.\", \"user\": \"persona_012\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780060080.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780060080.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Opening the third CrownPeak follow-up review: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 is sitting on account 219000 for northstar_legal and has been pending_approval since 2026-03-11 (81 days as of today). Tariq is pulling the line-level detail and I'm running the vendor-amount-billing-period match now. Anaya, can you pull the Q1 2026 IR engagement correspondence? The line memo references a Q1 IR contingency retainer pre-bill, so the engagement file is where I expect to find the cancellation breadcrumb if there is one.\", \"user\": \"npc_029\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1780069320.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779992820.885106\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Good. Let's move on that basis. Please keep the first version practical: minimal fields, clear approvals, and a short exception log. Once we have a draft, we can test it on a couple of managed clients and tighten anything that creates avoidable rework.\", \"user\": \"persona_023\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779992710.852812\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779992710.852812\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"That works for me. I can help draft the standard intake form and a checklist for the payment package, so we've got the same fields every time.\", \"user\": \"persona_010\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779992538.502965\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779992820.885106\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779992642.806929\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Yes - we should formalize three decision points: vendor onboarding, invoice approval, and payment release. I suggest one owner for each step, with a documented backup and a clear client approver matrix. If we keep exceptions to a minimum and require written sign-off for anything outside policy, the process will be much easier to manage consistently.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779992538.502965\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779992538.502965\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"@daniel.jones can you weigh in on the approval threshold piece? I'm comfortable with a standard intake and tracker, but I want to make sure the control points are strong enough without creating unnecessary friction for clients.\", \"user\": \"persona_023\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779992465.488485\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1779992710.852812\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779992465.488485\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Agreed. I'd also add a simple status tracker so we can tell at a glance what's waiting on vendor info vs client approval vs ready to pay. That would cut down on back-and-forth a lot.\", \"user\": \"npc_021\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779992400.325289\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779992538.502965\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779992400.325289\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I think we should standardize the AP workflow for managed clients around one intake path: vendor setup, approval matrix, payment method, and cutoff timing. Right now we're still handling too many one-off versions, and it's making review harder than it needs to be.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779992465.488485\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779817320.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Reviewed Owen's write-up and Ben's classification check. I agree CrownPeak VEN-030-817856 for apinv_178dc009be6c474f is a valid obligation that got stuck because it was coded to 210000 instead of 219000. Fix is to re-code 210000 -> 219000 and then release for payment. Owen, please attach the coding-classification clarification to the existing CrownPeak follow-up Linear ticket so we have the issue trail tied back to this review.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779455700.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779801720.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Scanned the rest of acct 210000 for FX-denominated lines that may have missed the April revaluation. Only item still sitting unrevalued is the Acme Research UK subscription line; the other two FX lines in the account were already picked up in April (one EUR vendor and one CAD vendor). I also cross-tied May USD/GBP at 0.7838 to the closing-day source feed to four decimals, so that rate breadcrumb is now documented here for the review trail.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779464880.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Duplicate check came back clean on apinv_178dc009be6c474f / CrownPeak VEN-030-817856. I matched vendor + amount + billing period across 2025 and found no paid CrownPeak sibling at $1,152.41. The invoice line memo reads \\\"IR retainer scope add  -  Jul 2025 incident,\\\" which supports this being a real July obligation rather than a duplicate. Separate issue: it is coded to 210000, but IR-service adds like this are usually routed to 219000, so we should review classification before release.\", \"user\": \"npc_029\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779455700.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779455700.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Starting follow-through review on the remaining CrownPeak items for northstar_legal. Main holdout is apinv_178dc009be6c474f, vendor invoice VEN-030-817856, $1,152.41 currently sitting on account 210000 and still pending approval after 326 days. I'm opening review threads on four points at once: whether this is a duplicate, what support we have tying it to the July 2025 billing period, whether it belongs in 210000 or should be reclassed to 219000, and whether we should release it once those are cleared.\", \"user\": \"npc_029\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1779817320.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779387840.407256\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Yeah, that's the right split. We can also flag exceptions in the template so the team isn't guessing.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779387755.179565\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779387755.179565\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"@andrea.phil can you sanity-check the approval step? I'm trying to figure out whether we should require client signoff on every invoice above a threshold, or just on new vendors and non-routine spend.\", \"user\": \"npc_029\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779387840.407256\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779387680.131138\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"For the intake, I'd keep it pretty simple: vendor list, approver matrix, payment terms, and any recurring invoices. If we collect that once and keep it current, it should cut down on back-and-forth later.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779387600.029698\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779387600.029698\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I want to tighten up the AP workflow for managed clients so we're not handling it ad hoc every time. Thinking a standard intake + approval path would help us keep vendor setup, invoice review, and payment timing consistent.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779387680.131138\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779217800.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Owen's duplicate-billing check, Tariq's parallel-history pull, and Brenda's vendor-side confirmation all support dismissing CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-353041 as a duplicate against paid invoice VEN-030-353009 for the March 2026 MSSP cycle. I'm taking this to Matthew Li for northstar_legal partner sign-off on the dismissal narrative. Owen, please open a Linear item that covers both the fuzzy-match rule tuning (Priya will own the rule decision once we have the proposal) for memo variants like 'Mar MSSP retainer' vs 'March MSSP monthly fee' and reviewing the two remaining long-pending CrownPeak items in the queue.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1778852700.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779215520.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Void-and-rebill is the right path here. We cannot pay against a deprecated SOW even if the underlying GraniteRack compute obligation is real. I'll take the partner ask to Steven Perry. Owen, please open a Linear ticket that covers both the rebill tracking for VEN-012-753165 and Brenda's idea to sweep the GraniteRack vendor-master description for any lingering SOW-2024-GR-rev3 references. Loop Lena in on the vendor-master sweep since the SOW lifecycle sits with procurement. Keep it as a tracking breadcrumb in project tooling without touching the accounting records yet.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1778850840.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779209700.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"This is the right shape for a vendor dispute: contract math matches the billed add-seat charge, but the provisioning logs show no additional 2025 seat grant and usage telemetry stayed at 5 active users the whole period. I'm taking the Pinecrest matter to Andrea Phil for formal sign-off on disputing VEN-006-193120 and keeping apinv_dff20c11abdc495c on hold pending that review. Owen, please open a Linear issue for dispute tracking and include a broader Pinecrest license-utilization audit so we capture whether this was a one-off billing error or a wider pattern.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1778853000.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779204300.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Closing the loop here: apinv_5e09decd035d4443 was approved for release in full per Andrea. Root cause was orphan-approver routing after the July 2025 departure, so this was workflow plumbing rather than a bill dispute. Mateo owns the routing fix and brookfield-wide pending_approval sweep, and the Linear ticket is already open for follow-up.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1778850300.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779196440.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I reviewed Brenda's email chain and agree with the split-payment path: release $17,825 now and hold $6,650.25 pending the credit memo resolution on VEN-010-514242. At 87 days pending approval this is already past our 60-day exception threshold, so please open a Linear ticket under AP workflow standardization for managed clients and track both the disposition and a 90-day SLA follow-up there.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1778850720.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779130200.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Agree with Brenda  -  if we release this, it needs to be conditioned on receipt of the updated W-9 by a hard date, not a soft commitment. I'm taking the TimeLedger Nexus item to Steven Perry for partner sign-off on that approach. Please open a Linear issue for the expired W-9 sweep follow-up as well so we don't lose the broader vendor cleanup behind invoice VEN-010-693199.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1778851320.000000\"}, {\"ts\": \"1778857500.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Pinecrest is on the IT app list, but adding a seat should still have triggered provisioning. I'm going to check the SSO grant logs and usage telemetry to confirm whether Brookfield ever activated that extra seat. Mateo, can you pull the Pinecrest contract terms in parallel so we can compare the billed seat pricing against what was actually provisioned? That should give us two clean trails to reconcile: IT provisioning evidence and vendor contract pricing evidence.\", \"user\": \"npc_029\", \"thread_ts\": \"1778853000.000000\"}], \"has_more\": true, \"response_metadata\": {\"next_cursor\": \"1778857500.000000|9fac732d5984522e912155a0a51fc055\"}}"}

import json,sys

        print(json.dumps(i))

\"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"todo\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Track Pinecrest vendor dispute and complete license-utilization audit\", \"description\": \"Open and manage the vendor-dispute workstream for Pinecrest Workflow Works invoice VEN-006-193120 tied to AP invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c for Brookfield.\\n\\nContext:\\n- Invoice amount is $1,040.63 for an additional Pinecrest seat billed across March through July 2025.\\n- Contract pricing supports the math for one incremental seat at $208.13 per month.\\n- IT evidence indicates zero additional SSO grants in 2025.\\n- Usage telemetry shows 5 active users across the billed period rather than 6.\\n- Daniel Jones is taking the dispute recommendation to Andrea Phil for formal sign-off.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- Record the evidence basis for vendor dispute in one place.\\n- Document sign-off outcome from Andrea Phil.\\n- Prepare dispute-tracking notes and next-step owner once approval is received.\\n- Complete a broader Pinecrest license-utilization audit to confirm whether any other months or seats were misbilled.\\n- Summarize recommended vendor outreach package for AP follow-up.\", \"priority\": 2, \"estimate\": 3, \"due_date\": \"2026-06-02\", \"labels\": [\"vendor dispute\", \"ap\", \"pinecrest\", \"license audit\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-19T17:01:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-27T18:51:54Z\"}, {\"id\": \"issue_0b05ec215a854a439472fb0c6bd3307b\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"persona_002\", \"state_id\": \"todo\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Run expired W-9 sweep across managed-client vendor master\", \"description\": \"Context\\nDuring triage of TimeLedger Nexus invoice VEN-010-693199 for $21,777.01 on acme_cloud, we confirmed the payment hold is tied to an expired vendor W-9 rather than an invoice-content issue. Daniel Jones directed that we log the broader follow-up so other vendors with stale tax documentation are not missed.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Pull a vendor-master report for managed clients showing W-9 expiration status.\\n- Identify all vendors with expired or missing W-9s.\\n- Prioritize any vendors tied to currently pending AP invoices.\\n- Propose a standard hard-deadline escalation path for conditioned payment release when a W-9 is stale.\\n- Return findings to AP ops with an owner list and next actions.\\n\\nRelated context\\nCurrent invoice under review: TimeLedger Nexus / VEN-010-693199 / $21,777.01 / entity acme_cloud.\", \"priority\": 2, \"estimate\": 3, \"due_date\": \"2026-05-30\", \"labels\": [\"ap-controls\", \"vendor-master\", \"w-9\", \"follow-up\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-18T18:56:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-27T18:40:13Z\"}, {\"id\": \"issue_16b223fecbf14af586d0afa0ce8cc6f4\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"todo\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Track GraniteRack void-and-rebill and vendor-master sweep\", \"description\": \"Context\\nDaniel Jones directed AP to pursue void-and-rebill for GraniteRack Compute Services invoice VEN-012-753165 for $39,090.56 because the invoice references deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 and cannot be paid as submitted, even though the underlying compute obligation is real and was accrued to account 219000. Brenda Abbas confirmed GraniteRack AR can void and reissue under SOW-2025-GR-rev1 in the next billing cycle targeting 2026-06-05, and also flagged that the GraniteRack vendor-master description may still reference the deprecated SOW. Lena Park confirmed from procurement that the supersession went effective 2026-01-15, so the vendor-master entry is stale rather than ambiguous. Priya Khatri signed off on void-and-rebill as the AP-policy disposition.\\n\\nScope\\nTrack the rebill follow-up for the original AP exception tied to apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e and document a sweep of GraniteRack vendor-master descriptions across managed entities to identify any lingering SOW-2024-GR-rev3 references.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Confirm owner and expected date for GraniteRack void and reissue of VEN-012-753165 under SOW-2025-GR-rev1.\\n- Record the original invoice amount of $39,090.56 and link this work back to the AP exception context (85 days in pending_approval as of universe cutoff).\\n- Inventory Brookfield GraniteRack vendor-master description references that still cite SOW-2024-GR-rev3, with Lena Park owning the procurement-side cleanup.\\n- Note any additional entities or recurring invoice risk discovered during the sweep.\\n- Keep this as a tracking breadcrumb only; no accounting-system write or approval action is taken from this issue.\\n\\nRequested by Daniel Jones; Owen Mercer to open and coordinate, Lena Park on vendor-master cleanup.\", \"priority\": 2, \"estimate\": 3, \"due_date\": \"2026-06-05\", \"labels\": [\"accounts-payable\", \"vendor-master\", \"graniterack\", \"void-and-rebill\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-19T18:38:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-27T18:36:36Z\"}, {\"id\": \"linear_6e80862770a3\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"linear_2e7b3ea28766\", \"state_id\": null, \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": 3114, \"title\": \"AP standardization - late vendor approval chain caused miss on MetroShield and Union Square queue\", \"description\": \"Use recent misses to tighten the AP workflow standard. Review how invoice VEN-012-211225 from MetroShield Facility Services ($4,726.05, approved) and invoice VEN-014-300659 from Union Square Realty Partners ($6,379.51, approved) moved through approval and why they were not released within the expected pay cycle. Update handoff rules, exception thresholds, and invoice support requirements for managed clients with decentralized approvers.\", \"priority\": 2, \"estimate\": 5, \"due_date\": \"2026-05-14\", \"labels\": [\"active_project\", \"ap-workflow\", \"process-design\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-10T08:49:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-12T16:35:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"linear_6186144f3d1e\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"linear_6ef0fc68abd7\", \"state_id\": null, \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": 3107, \"title\": \"vendor-583136: AP hold - pending approval chain review for VEN-019-583136\", \"description\": \"Standardization follow-up for managed client AP process. Invoice VEN-019-583136 from Bridgefield Backup Systems ($5,279.87, pending_approval) surfaced in the late vendor approval chain review and appears to have stalled between budget owner and controller sign-off. Map the missed handoff, document approval threshold treatment, and update the standard workflow notes so similar technology invoices do not miss the pay run.\", \"priority\": 1, \"estimate\": 5, \"due_date\": \"2026-04-08\", \"labels\": [\"active_project\", \"ap-workflow\", \"vendor-approval\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-04-04T08:55:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-06T13:29:00Z\"}]"}

[{"id": "issue_2616eb556d5e42aa9c1360e54c5fc00e", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "todo", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Track Pinecrest vendor dispute and complete license-utilization audit", "description": "Open and manage the vendor-dispute workstream for Pinecrest Workflow Works invoice VEN-006-193120 tied to AP invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c for Brookfield.

Context:
- Invoice amount is $1,040.63 for an additional Pinecrest seat billed across March through July 2025.
- Contract pricing supports the math for one incremental seat at $208.13 per month.
- IT evidence indicates zero additional SSO grants in 2025.
- Usage telemetry shows 5 active users across the billed period rather than 6.
- Daniel Jones is taking the dispute recommendation to Andrea Phil for formal sign-off.

Acceptance criteria:
- Record the evidence basis for vendor dispute in one place.
- Document sign-off outcome from Andrea Phil.
- Prepare dispute-tracking notes and next-step owner once approval is received.
- Complete a broader Pinecrest license-utilization audit to confirm whether any other months or seats were misbilled.
- Summarize recommended vendor outreach package for AP follow-up.", "priority": 2, "estimate": 3, "due_date": "2026-06-02", "labels": ["vendor dispute", "ap", "pinecrest", "license audit"], "links": [], "is_archived": false, "created_at": "2026-05-19T17:01:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-27T18:51:54Z"}, {"id": "issue_0b05ec215a854a439472fb0c6bd3307b", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "persona_002", "state_id": "todo", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Run expired W-9 sweep across managed-client vendor master", "description": "Context
During triage of TimeLedger Nexus invoice VEN-010-693199 for $21,777.01 on acme_cloud, we confirmed the payment hold is tied to an expired vendor W-9 rather than an invoice-content issue. Daniel Jones directed that we log the broader follow-up so other vendors with stale tax documentation are not missed.

Acceptance criteria
- Pull a vendor-master report for managed clients showing W-9 expiration status.
- Identify all vendors with expired or missing W-9s.
- Prioritize any vendors tied to currently pending AP invoices.
- Propose a standard hard-deadline escalation path for conditioned payment release when a W-9 is stale.
- Return findings to AP ops with an owner list and next actions.

Related context

Daniel Jones directed AP to pursue void-and-rebill for GraniteRack Compute Services invoice VEN-012-753165 for $39,090.56 because the invoice references deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 and cannot be paid as submitted, even though the underlying compute obligation is real and was accrued to account 219000. Brenda Abbas confirmed GraniteRack AR can void and reissue under SOW-2025-GR-rev1 in the next billing cycle targeting 2026-06-05, and also flagged that the GraniteRack vendor-master description may still reference the deprecated SOW. Lena Park confirmed from procurement that the supersession went effective 2026-01-15, so the vendor-master entry is stale rather than ambiguous. Priya Khatri signed off on void-and-rebill as the AP-policy disposition.

Scope
Track the rebill follow-up for the original AP exception tied to apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e and document a sweep of GraniteRack vendor-master descriptions across managed entities to identify any lingering SOW-2024-GR-rev3 references.

Acceptance criteria
- Confirm owner and expected date for GraniteRack void and reissue of VEN-012-753165 under SOW-2025-GR-rev1.
- Record the original invoice amount of $39,090.56 and link this work back to the AP exception context (85 days in pending_approval as of universe cutoff).
- Inventory Brookfield GraniteRack vendor-master description references that still cite SOW-2024-GR-rev3, with Lena Park owning the procurement-side cleanup.
- Note any additional entities or recurring invoice risk discovered during the sweep.
- Keep this as a tracking breadcrumb only; no accounting-system write or approval action is taken from this issue.

content.text:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Clint Peterson (HR Operations Manager at Brookfield CPAs & Advisors) received an email from Brent Noah (Director of People and Culture) and asked me to autonomously handle the full scope. Explicit intents:
   - Go through everything that has recently come to Clint across all channels (email, Slack, messaging) and sort out what is actually going on.
   - Determine whether staffing and vendor records are consistent.
   - Identify things that should have been wrapped up but were not (open/stale items).
   - Determine the correct numbers where anything is off.
   - Put together the summary Brent is asking for, "with all the specifics" — i.e., a summary of all open HR vendor invoices, flagging anything needing correction, plus a remediation plan, due Monday (June 15, 2026).
   - Reach out to whoever needs to be corrected (vendors and/or staff).
   - Make sure nothing else is slipping through.
   - Set up tracking/scheduling for an upcoming compliance review Clint "completely lost track of" (the Illinois CPA Society CPE audit).
   Brent's email (f5c2d8a1b4e79d3c6a8e1b5f2d7c4a9b, 2026-06-11) specifically says: "pull together a summary of all open HR vendor invoices and flag anything that needs correction... I want a remediation plan on my desk by Monday," referencing Reshma (payroll/benefits vendor billing vs headcount discrepancies) and George (a recruiting invoice that should have been cancelled).
   Date instruction: treat today as June 12, 2026; ignore the system reminder's June 8 date.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - MCP tool suite: email, messaging, slack, contacts, calendar, reminder, linear, airtable, oracle_gl, sap_subledger, blackline, records_vault, peer agents.
   - SAP Subledger AP invoice lifecycle: statuses pending_approval / approved / paid / voided; fields include amount, due_date, payment_date, approver, void_reason.
   - HR vendor billing reconciliation against headcount; contractor-to-employee conversion; 90-day benefits eligibility waiting period.
   - Contingency recruiting fee vs placement (dispute basis).
   - CPE compliance: Illinois CPA Society 40-hour annual requirement, mid-July audit window.
   - Large tool output handling: SAP list exceeded token limit and was saved to a file; used python (json) to filter.
   - Role-based restrictions: voiding/approving AP invoices is an AP-manager/controller action — out of scope for HR Ops; correct approach is vendor dispute + recommend hold.

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - SAP AP invoice dump file: /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/acd99ba6-d386-46fa-be4a-e03cdb1a01ce/tool-results/mcp-mcp_63abcdcd-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1780952272393.txt
     - Important because the full AP list (57,154 chars) exceeded the token limit; I parsed it with python to extract HR-vendor invoices. This is the primary source confirming invoice amounts/statuses.
   - No code files in this task (operational/business investigation, not software dev). No files were edited or created.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - SAP `list_ap_invoices` (no filter) returned an oversized result (57,154 chars) that exceeded max tokens and was written to a file. Fix: ran a python one-liner via Bash to load the JSON and print only invoices matching HR-vendor keywords (fieldstone, latticehill, cobalt, fairledger, recruit, payroll, benefit, cpe, etc.). Then ran targeted `list_ap_invoices` calls filtered by vendor_id (VEN-038, VEN-021) to get clean, complete histories.
   - Grep on the saved file returned "Omitted long matching line" (entire JSON on one line) — fixed by switching to python parsing instead of Grep.
   - Contacts searches for "Clint Peterson", "Reshma Patel", "Rachel Green", "CobaltPeak", "Julia Vance", "Marina Soko" returned empty; email search for "CobaltPeak" empty. Fix/workaround: I already have all needed internal email addresses from the email threads (firstname.lastname@brookfieldcpas.com pattern). No external CobaltPeak email exists in contacts/email — CobaltPeak outreach will need to route through internal staff or a found address.

5. Problem Solving:
   Confirmed and quantified four HR vendor invoice issues against the SAP primary source (not just secondhand Slack/email references), per the instruction to go to source systems:
   - Fieldstone Recruiting VEN-038-703291 (apinv_d9f5a7c3b1e24e42): $4,850, pending_approval, due 2026-07-02. Senior Accountant search initiated March 2026; Reshma verbally cancelled mid-April after firm decided to promote internally; George confirms position filled internally after an April resignation; NO written cancellation confirmation obtained. Vendor (Marcus Webb) claims 8 candidates sourced before cancellation so fee applies per terms. Our position: dispute full $4,850 (no placement made); correct amount = $0 pending production of engagement terms. Recommend HOLD (do not approve).
   - LatticeHill Learning VEN-033-612488 (apinv_c7e3a5d1b9f04c84): $2,590, pending_approval, due 2026-06-28. Billed 14 registered × $185; only 11 attended. 3 cancelled after the 48-hour registration lock: Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, Steven Perry. Overbilled by 3 × $185 = $555; correct amount = $2,035. Recommend HOLD and request amended invoice/credit.
   - CobaltPeak Benefits VEN-021-450891 (apinv_b5f1d9c3a7e84b26): $3,847.55, APPROVED by julia.vance but UNPAID, due 2026-07-03. Risk: 3 contractor-to-employee conversions (mid-April, operations support pool) may have been wrongly enrolled in firm benefits despite a 90-day eligibility waiting period (ends ~mid-July). Needs the 3 names (Rachel pulling them) + verification with CobaltPeak. Recommend HOLD payment pending verification; request credit if enrolled prematurely. Still interceptable since unpaid.
   - FairLedger Payroll VEN-020-842917 (apinv_a3d7c1e5b9f24d68): $6,210.40, pending_approval, due 2026-07-05. May 2026 processing fee; headcount 143 active employees correctly includes the 3 conversions (they SHOULD be on payroll). Consistent — no correction.
   Core consistency finding: the 3 conversions belong on PAYROLL (FairLedger — correct) but NOT on BENEFITS (CobaltPeak — until ~mid-July). Payroll headcount (143) reconciles; benefits is the exposure.
   CPE connection: the 3 CPE no-shows (Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, Steven Perry) missed the May 28 Annual Ethics & Regulatory Update (4 credits) and may now be short on the 40-hour CPE requirement for the Illinois audit.
   Existing reminder discovered: reminder_c5a9d1e3b7f4 "CPE audit preparation - compile training attendance records for Illinois CPA Society review," due 2026-06-15T10:00:00Z, already covers the CPE prep — must avoid duplicating it; may augment with additional calendar/reminders and a status reply to Marina.

6. All user messages:
   - The single substantive user message (the task brief): "Brent sent me something yesterday about our people operations and whether things are running the way they should be on the vendor and staffing side. I have not had a chance to properly read it yet but from the tone he wants a full accounting and a plan to address whatever is off, and he wants it by Monday. I have been completely tied up in the employee file policy work for weeks... People have been sending me messages and flagging issues... I have not followed up on any of it... Can you go through everything... sort out what is actually going on? I need to know if our staffing and vendor records are consistent, if there are things that should have been wrapped up but were not, and what the right numbers should be where anything is off. Once you have it figured out, put together the summary Brent is asking for with all the specifics, reach out to whoever needs to be corrected, and make sure nothing else is slipping through. There is also something about a compliance review coming up that I keep hearing about but have completely lost track of. Whatever needs to be tracked or scheduled going forward, set that up too. I do not want to be scrambling when Monday comes."
   - System reminders about MCP servers, available skills, and the current date context.
   - The final message: instruction to produce this text-only summary with no tool calls.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Reach out to vendors/staff needing correction:
     - Email Fieldstone (accounts@fieldstonerecruiting.com, Marcus Webb) disputing VEN-038-703291 ($4,850), citing April cancellation + internal fill, requesting written cancellation confirmation and engagement terms; cc Reshma + George. State invoice will not be approved pending resolution.
     - Email LatticeHill (invoices@latticehilllearning.com) disputing VEN-033-612488 ($2,590), requesting amended invoice/credit for 3 no-shows (correct = $2,035 / $555 credit); cc Rachel + Reshma.
     - Handle CobaltPeak VEN-021-450891: get the 3 conversion names from Rachel, verify enrollment, flag/hold payment (alert Julia Vance/AP), request credit if enrolled prematurely.
     - Reply to Reshma (confirm Fieldstone handled via email for paper trail; coordinate) and Rachel (urgently request 3 names; confirm FairLedger vs CobaltPeak reconciliation).
   - Draft and send Brent's HR vendor remediation summary + plan (by Monday June 15) covering all four vendors with specifics and recommended corrections/numbers.
   - Set up CPE audit tracking/scheduling: send status update to Marina Soko (and Rachel; due end of next week ~June 19), possibly schedule a prep meeting, add calendar entries/reminders (without duplicating existing reminder_c5a9d1e3b7f4), and flag the 3 CPE no-shows as potential gap candidates needing make-up sessions.
   - "Make sure nothing else is slipping through": consider creating Linear tracking tickets (likely under project linear_63697b7dff5c, AP workflow standardization) for the open vendor items.
   - Update the TodoWrite list as items complete.

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary I had completed the investigation/verification phase entirely. I had just run a parallel batch: contacts searches (CobaltPeak, Julia Vance, Marina Soko — all empty), `reminder_get_all_reminders` (which surfaced the pre-existing CPE prep reminder reminder_c5a9d1e3b7f4 due 2026-06-15), and an email search for CobaltPeak (empty). At this point all four HR vendor invoices were confirmed against SAP with amounts, statuses, due dates, and the correct/disputed numbers determined. No outbound actions (emails, reminders, calendar events, Linear tickets, or the Brent summary) had yet been taken. The todo list stood at: item 1 completed; item 2 (Fieldstone) in_progress; items 3–9 pending.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Proceed to the action phase that the user explicitly requested ("put together the summary Brent is asking for... reach out to whoever needs to be corrected... set that up too"). Concretely, the next step is to begin executing outreach and deliverables: send the vendor dispute emails (Fieldstone VEN-038-703291 $4,850; LatticeHill VEN-033-612488 correct to $2,035/$555 credit), reply to Rachel requesting the 3 conversion names and to Reshma confirming the email-based Fieldstone handling, flag the CobaltPeak VEN-021-450891 hold, then compose and send Brent's full summary + remediation plan, and set up CPE audit tracking (status to Marina, calendar/reminders without duplicating reminder_c5a9d1e3b7f4). This is directly in line with the user's request that "Once you have it figured out, put together the summary Brent is asking for with all the specifics, reach out to whoever needs to be corrected, and make sure nothing else is slipping through... Whatever needs to be tracked or scheduled going forward, set that up too."

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/acd99ba6-d386-46fa-be4a-e03cdb1a01ce.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

We sourced and screened 8 candidates before being advised of a potential cancellation in mid-April. As sourcing work had already been completed by that point, the recruitment fee applies per our engagement terms.\\n\\nInvoice: VEN-038-703291\\nAmount: $4,850.00\\nTerms: Net 30\\n\\nPlease let us know if you have any questions.\\n\\nBest,\\nMarcus Webb\\nFieldstone Recruiting Partners\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-03T16:10:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0009\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition recommendation for exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d on BL-75810CD0FEE4\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nApproved to accept timing for FP-2026-05 on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d tied to BL-75810CD0FEE4 and account 119000. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.\\n\\nThe CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.\\n\\nMonday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-31T14:19:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_009_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition approval request: exc_aade06f6129e43 for FP-2026-05 cash-payroll feed drop\", \"content\": \"Hi Blue,\\n\\nApproved. Accept-timing for FP-2026-05 is fine based on the 29 dropped rows, Ryan's note, George's precedent, and Hannah's downstream review.\\n\\nIf the June 2 retry does not clear, re-verify at FP-2026-06 BD3 and confirm whether anything changed in the feed behavior before we revisit disposition. Until then, leave exc_aade06f6129e43 in logged state.\\n\\nThanks,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T19:51:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue on 131000 due May 30\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nPartner-sight noted on the $6,068.91 amount and Daniel's proposed accounting path.\\n\\nWhat stands out to me is not just the duplicate posting but the vendor-management gap behind the May 22 manual re-key. If a renewal can be manually re-entered without clear confirmation of the active subscription footprint, that points to a broader controls concern around how vendor changes are being validated before they hit the books.\\n\\nNo change from my side on the accounting mechanics Daniel approved. Please keep the exception in investigating until the correction is prepared and the side-workstream is closed through the IT/CIO check.\\n\\nBest,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T12:27:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue on 131000 due May 30\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nApproved on the disposition path.\\n\\nUse FP-2026-05 and book the correction as DR Accrued Vendor Payments 219500 and CR Prepaid Software Subscriptions 131000 for $6,068.91. This should reverse the May 22 manual re-key rather than touch the May 15 auto-renewal entry.\\n\\nI will handle the IT/CIO follow-up on seat count and make sure we confirm there is only one active subscription population going forward. Keep the exception in investigating until the entry is actually prepared and we have that vendor-side confirmation in hand.\\n\\nThanks,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T12:25:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition\", \"content\": \"Hi Owen,\\n\\nI heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.\\n\\nTheir AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. I now have full confirmation. Key facts verified:
- **Fieldstone** VEN-038-703291 ($4,850): Reshma cancelled by phone mid-April, no written confirmation; filled internally; vendor claiming sourcing fee.
- **LatticeHill** VEN-033-612488 ($2,590): 14 billed, 11 attended; 3 no-shows = Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, Steven Perry (locked-in after 48hr cutoff). Correct = $2,035, $555 credit.
- **CobaltPeak** VEN-021-450891 ($3,847.55): approved by Julia Vance, unpaid — 3 April contractor conversions may be prematurely enrolled (90-day waiting period to ~mid-July). Rachel still pulling the 3 names.
- **FairLedger** VEN-020-842917 ($6,210.40): 143 employees incl. conversions — correct, no action.

Sending vendor disputes and internal coordination now.

input.subject: Invoice VEN-038-703291 - disputed (search cancelled, no placement)
input.content:
Marcus,

I'm following up on invoice VEN-038-703291 for $4,850.00 covering the Senior Accountant search initiated in March 2026. Brookfield is disputing this invoice, and it will not be approved for payment in its current form.

Our records show the search was cancelled by phone in mid-April 2026 when we decided to fill the role through an internal promotion. The position was filled internally and no Fieldstone candidate was placed. Reshma Patel, who placed the cancellation call, was advised at the time that your system would be updated, but we never received written confirmation of the cancellation.

Before we take any further action on this invoice, please provide:

1. A copy of the signed engagement terms / fee agreement for this search, specifically the clause you are relying on to bill a fee where no placement was made.
2. Written confirmation of the date our cancellation request was received and logged on your side.
3. An itemized record of the sourcing work referenced (the 8 candidates), with dates, so we can confirm what was performed before our mid-April cancellation.

To be clear: no placement was made and the engagement was cancelled. Absent contractual terms that unambiguously support a sourcing fee on a no-placement cancellation, we expect this invoice to be withdrawn. Please direct all further correspondence on this matter to me.

Regards,
Clint Peterson
HR Operations Manager
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors

input.subject: Invoice #VEN-033-612488 - attendance correction required (May 28 CPE session)
input.content:
To the LatticeHill billing team,

I'm writing regarding invoice VEN-033-612488 for $2,590.00 for the Annual Ethics and Regulatory Update CPE session delivered on May 28, 2026. The invoice bills 14 registered attendees at $185 each. Three of the registered staff cancelled and did not attend. Because the cancellations came after your 48-hour registration lock, they remained on the registration list, but they were not present and did not receive the training.

Actual attendance was 11, not 14. We are requesting an amended invoice (or credit memo) reflecting 11 attendees:

- 11 attendees x $185 = $2,035.00 (corrected total)
- Overbilled amount: 3 x $185 = $555.00 (credit due)

We will hold approval of this invoice pending a corrected invoice or credit memo. Separately, please let us know how you would like to handle cancellations that fall inside the 48-hour lock as a matter of policy, so we can avoid this on future sessions.

Please send the amended invoice to me and cc Rachel Green.

Regards,
Clint Peterson
HR Operations Manager
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors

input.subject: Please hold payment - CobaltPeak invoice VEN-021-450891 (possible premature benefits enrollment)
input.content:
Hi Julia,

Flagging a benefits-billing issue on CobaltPeak Benefits invoice VEN-021-450891 ($3,847.55), which I see was approved on the AP side but has not yet been paid (due 2026-07-03). I'd like to request that payment be held pending verification.

Background: in mid-April we converted three operations-support contractors to full employees through FairLedger payroll. Their contractor agreements specify a 90-day benefits eligibility waiting period, so they should not be enrolled in firm benefits until that period ends (approximately mid-July). Rachel Green flagged that she is not certain whether CobaltPeak was updated correctly - i.e., whether these three were enrolled in benefits prematurely.

If they were enrolled early, the CobaltPeak invoice is overstated and we will need a credit. I'm working with Rachel to get the three names and will verify enrollment directly with CobaltPeak. Because the invoice is approved but still unpaid, we can intercept this before cash goes out.

Could you place this invoice on payment hold until I confirm enrollment is correct? I'll follow up by end of next week (June 19) with the verification result.

To be clear on the payroll side: FairLedger's May processing correctly reflects these three on payroll (143 active employees), which is where they belong. The issue is benefits eligibility only.

Thanks,
Clint Peterson
HR Operations Manager
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors

Rachel - I need those three conversion names as soon as you can pull them. I've flagged the CobaltPeak invoice (VEN-021-450891, $3,847.55) for a payment hold with Julia Vance since it's approved but not yet paid, so we have a window to fix it before cash goes out. Once you send the names I'll verify directly with CobaltPeak whether they were enrolled in benefits before the 90-day waiting period ends (~mid-July) and request a credit if they were. The FairLedger payroll side looks correct - their May confirmation shows 143 active employees including the three conversions, which is right; the exposure is benefits only. Separately, the three CPE no-shows from May 28 (Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, Steven Perry) may now be short on hours for the Illinois CPA Society audit - I'm tracking that and will need to confirm whether make-up sessions are needed before mid-July.

Reshma - update on both items. (1) Fieldstone: I've sent them a written dispute on VEN-038-703291 ($4,850), cc'ing you and George, asking them to produce the signed engagement terms and proof of when your April cancellation was logged. Since no placement was made and we filled internally, our position is the fee does not apply and the invoice should be withdrawn. (2) LatticeHill: I've emailed billing to correct the May 28 CPE invoice down to 11 attendees ($2,035, a $555 credit) for the three who cancelled inside the 48-hour lock. When you have the attended-vs-registered list, send it over so I have it on file for both the LatticeHill credit and the Illinois CPE audit. Thanks for flagging both.

input.subject: Re: HR vendor billing review - summary, corrections, and remediation plan
input.content:
Brent,

Here is the full accounting of open HR vendor invoices you asked for, with the issues flagged, the correct numbers, and a remediation plan. I have already actioned the vendor-facing and internal pieces (detail below). All figures are confirmed against the SAP AP subledger, not just secondhand.

=====================================================
OPEN HR VENDOR INVOICES - SUMMARY
=====================================================

There are four open HR vendor invoices. Two need correction, one needs a payment hold pending verification, one is clean.

1. FIELDSTONE RECRUITING PARTNERS - INVOICE VEN-038-703291
   - Billed: $4,850.00 | Status: pending approval | Due: 2026-07-02
   - Issue: Senior Accountant search initiated March 2026, cancelled by phone in mid-April when we filled the role via internal promotion (George McAdam's team, following an April resignation). No candidate was ever placed. Reshma cancelled verbally but did not obtain written confirmation. Fieldstone is now billing a full fee, claiming 8 candidates were sourced before cancellation.
   - Correct amount: $0 (no placement made). At most, this is a contractual question that hinges on engagement terms we have not yet seen.
   - Action taken: I sent Fieldstone a written dispute today (cc George and Reshma) demanding the signed engagement terms, the clause they rely on, proof of when our cancellation was logged, and dated evidence of the sourcing work. The invoice will not be approved pending resolution.
   - Exposure if unaddressed: $4,850.

2. LATTICEHILL LEARNING CENTER - INVOICE VEN-033-612488
   - Billed: $2,590.00 | Status: pending approval | Due: 2026-06-28
   - Issue: May 28 "Annual Ethics & Regulatory Update" CPE session billed 14 registered attendees x $185. Only 11 attended. Three staff (Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, Steven Perry) cancelled after LatticeHill's 48-hour registration lock, so they stayed on the roster but did not attend.
   - Correct amount: $2,035.00 (11 x $185). Overbilled by $555.00.
   - Action taken: I emailed LatticeHill billing today (cc Rachel and Reshma) requesting an amended invoice / credit memo to $2,035, and asked them to clarify their inside-48-hour cancellation policy. Approval is on hold pending the corrected invoice.
   - Exposure if unaddressed: $555.

3. COBALTPEAK BENEFITS ADMINISTRATORS - INVOICE VEN-021-450891
   - Billed: $3,847.55 | Status: APPROVED (by Julia Vance) but NOT yet paid | Due: 2026-07-03
   - Issue: In mid-April we converted three operations-support contractors to full employees. Their agreements carry a 90-day benefits eligibility waiting period (ends ~mid-July), so they should not be on firm benefits yet. Rachel is not certain CobaltPeak was updated correctly and they may have been enrolled prematurely - which would overstate this invoice.
   - Correct amount: TBD - cannot confirm until we verify enrollment. If the three were enrolled early, we are owed a credit.
   - Action taken: Because the invoice is approved but unpaid, it is still interceptable. I asked Julia Vance to place it on payment hold and am getting the three names from Rachel to verify enrollment directly with CobaltPeak. Verification target: June 19.
   - Exposure if unaddressed: potential partial overbilling within the $3,847.55; full amount at risk of going out the door before we verify.

4. FAIRLEDGER PAYROLL SERVICES - INVOICE VEN-020-842917
   - Billed: $6,210.40 | Status: pending approval | Due: 2026-07-05
   - Assessment: CLEAN. May 2026 processing fee at 143 active employees, which correctly includes the three April conversions (they belong on payroll). Headcount reconciles. No correction needed.

=====================================================
THE CORE CONSISTENCY FINDING
=====================================================
The three April contractor-to-employee conversions are the thread connecting payroll and benefits. They are correctly on PAYROLL (FairLedger - right) but must NOT be on BENEFITS (CobaltPeak) until the 90-day waiting period ends. Payroll headcount reconciles; the benefits enrollment is the open exposure. That single timing rule is what makes FairLedger correct and CobaltPeak suspect.

Total open HR vendor billing under review: $17,497.95.
Confirmed/likely corrections so far: $4,850 (Fieldstone, disputed in full) + $555 (LatticeHill overbilling) + an undetermined CobaltPeak amount.

=====================================================
REMEDIATION PLAN
=====================================================
Immediate (done today):
- Fieldstone dispute issued; invoice held.
- LatticeHill correction requested; invoice held.
- CobaltPeak payment-hold requested with Julia Vance; names being pulled.
- Reshma and Rachel looped in with clear next steps.

By June 19:
- Obtain the three conversion names from Rachel; verify CobaltPeak enrollment; request credit if enrolled early; release or correct the invoice accordingly.
- Receive LatticeHill amended invoice ($2,035) and route for approval.
- Receive Fieldstone's engagement terms; resolve dispute (target: withdrawal). Escalate to you/Legal if they refuse.
- Get Reshma's attended-vs-registered list on file for the LatticeHill credit and the CPE audit.

Process fixes to prevent recurrence:
- No verbal-only vendor cancellations: require written confirmation, logged, before a search/engagement is treated as closed (this is exactly what cost us the Fieldstone paper trail).
- HR vendor records (payroll, benefits) must be reconciled to headcount each month at conversion/onboarding events, with benefits eligibility dates explicitly tracked against the 90-day rule.
- Training invoices reconciled to actual attendance before approval, with the inside-48-hour cancellation policy documented.
- I'm opening tracking tickets for each open item so none of these slip again.

=====================================================
RELATED RISK - ILLINOIS CPA SOCIETY CPE AUDIT (mid-July)
=====================================================
Surfaced during this review: the three LatticeHill no-shows (Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, Steven Perry) missed a 4-credit ethics/regulatory session and may now be short on the 40-hour annual CPE requirement the Illinois CPA Society audit will sample. I'm compiling attendance records with Rachel and Marina Soko and will schedule make-up sessions before the audit window if any are short. Status update to Marina is due June 19; I'm setting up the tracking and a prep meeting separately.

I'll have any open threads buttoned up early next week. Happy to walk through any of this.

Clint
Clint Peterson
HR Operations Manager
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors

input.title: Illinois CPA Society CPE audit prep - attendance records & gap remediation
input.description:
Illinois CPA Society CPE audit window opens mid-July 2026; they will sample professional staff to verify the 40-hour annual CPE requirement. Owners: Clint Peterson (HR Ops) + Rachel Green; oversight: Marina Soko.

**Due to Marina by 2026-06-19:** confirm training attendance records compiled and gaps identified.

Tasks:
- Compile/consolidate training attendance records for all professional staff.
- Reconcile each staff member's YTD CPE hours against the 40-hour requirement.
- Identified gap candidates: Matthew Li, Andrea Phil, Steven Perry - registered but did NOT attend the May 28 Annual Ethics & Regulatory Update (4 credits); verify YTD totals and whether they are now short.
- Schedule make-up sessions before the audit window for any staff short on hours.
- Prep meeting scheduled with Marina + Rachel for 2026-06-16.

Cross-reference: the same 3 staff drive the LatticeHill invoice correction (VEN-033-612488).

content.text:
Everything is done. Here's the full accounting of what I found and what I've actioned on your behalf.

## What was actually going on

I went through your email, Slack, and direct messages and traced every HR-vendor reference back to the SAP AP subledger (not just what people said). Four open HR vendor invoices, $17,497.95 total. The common thread is the **three contractor-to-employee conversions** Rachel processed in mid-April — they belong on payroll but not yet on benefits (90-day waiting period runs to ~mid-July). That single rule explains which invoices are right and which are wrong.

| Vendor / Invoice | Amount | Status | Finding | Correct # |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Fieldstone** VEN-038-703291 | $4,850.00 | pending | Search cancelled by phone mid-April, filled internally, no placement; no written cancellation. Vendor billing full fee. | **$0 — disputed** |
| **LatticeHill** VEN-033-612488 | $2,590.00 | pending | Billed 14, only 11 attended (Li, Phil, Perry cancelled inside the 48hr lock). | **$2,035 ($555 credit)** |
| **CobaltPeak** VEN-021-450891 | $3,847.55 | **approved, unpaid** | 3 conversions may be enrolled in benefits prematurely. Still interceptable. | **TBD — held pending verification** |
| **FairLedger** VEN-020-842917 | $6,210.40 | pending | 143 employees incl. conversions — correct. | Clean, no action |

## What I did (all sent today)
- **Fieldstone** — written dispute demanding engagement terms + cancellation log; cc'd George and Reshma. Invoice held.
- **LatticeHill** — correction request to $2,035 / $555 credit; cc'd Rachel and Reshma.
- **CobaltPeak** — asked Julia Vance (the approver) to **put the payment on hold** before cash goes out; getting the 3 names from Rachel to verify enrollment.
- **Replied to Reshma and Rachel** with clear next steps and the name request.
- **Brent** — full summary + remediation plan with all specifics, timeline (June 19), and the process fixes (no verbal-only cancellations; monthly payroll/benefits-to-headcount reconciliation with eligibility tracking; reconcile training invoices to attendance).

## Compliance review you'd lost track of
The Illinois CPA Society **CPE audit** opens mid-July (40-hr requirement). I:
- Replied to **Marina** with a status update and flagged that the same three no-shows (Li, Phil, Perry) may now be short on hours.
- Scheduled a **prep meeting** with Marina + Rachel for **June 16**.
- Added two reminders (vendor chase **June 18**; CPE status to Marina **June 19**) — without duplicating the existing June 15 CPE-prep reminder.

## So nothing slips
Two Linear tickets on the Compliance team: **COMP-3116** (vendor remediation, all four invoices) and **COMP-3117** (CPE audit prep), both due June 19.

**Open dependencies for you next week:** Rachel's three conversion names (drives the CobaltPeak verification), LatticeHill's amended invoice, and Fieldstone's engagement terms — all reminder-tracked. You won't be scrambling Monday.
